questions.q: In which decade did Kishore Kumar become the dominant male playback voice of Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar rose to become the leading male playback singer of Hindi films during the 1970s.

questions.q: How many Filmfare Awards for Best Male Playback Singer did Kishore Kumar win during his career?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar won eight Filmfare Awards for Best Male Playback Singer, a record at the time.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar was famous for having no formal training in which discipline?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar had no formal classical music training, yet became one of India's greatest playback singers.

questions.q: In which year was the singer and actor Kishore Kumar born?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was born on 4 August 1929 in Khandwa, in present-day Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Besides singing, in which other roles in cinema was Kishore Kumar active?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was a multi-talented figure who worked as a singer, actor and music composer.

questions.q: With which famous music director did Kishore Kumar form one of his most celebrated singer-composer partnerships?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar's collaboration with R. D. Burman produced many of the most popular songs of the 1970s.

questions.q: For which superstar, often called the first Hindi cinema superstar, did Kishore Kumar famously provide playback in the early 1970s?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar became the defining playback voice for Rajesh Khanna, especially in films like Aradhana.

questions.q: In which Indian town was the singer Kishore Kumar born?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was born in Khandwa, a town in present-day Madhya Pradesh, in 1929.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar also sang playback for which 1970s 'angry young man' superstar in films such as Don and Sharaabi?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar provided playback for Amitabh Bachchan in hits like Don and Sharaabi.

questions.q: What was the surname adopted by Kishore Kumar and his famous elder brother, the actor Ashok?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was born Abhas Kumar Ganguly, and his elder brother was the celebrated actor Ashok Kumar (Ganguly).

questions.q: What was the birth name of the singer popularly known as Kishore Kumar?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was born Abhas Kumar Ganguly in 1929.

questions.q: The hugely popular Kishore Kumar solo song 'Mere Sapno Ki Rani', picturised on Rajesh Khanna, comes from which 1969 film?
questions.explanation: 'Mere Sapno Ki Rani', a Kishore Kumar solo, is from the 1969 film Aradhana, which marked his major comeback as a singer.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar starred alongside his brothers Ashok and Anoop in which acclaimed 1958 comedy directed by Satyen Bose?
questions.explanation: Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi (1958), directed by Satyen Bose, starred Kishore Kumar with his brothers Ashok and Anoop and remains a beloved comedy.

questions.q: For which 1969 film did Kishore Kumar win his first Filmfare Award for Best Male Playback Singer?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar won his first Filmfare Best Male Playback Singer award for the song 'Roop Tera Mastana' from Aradhana.

questions.q: In the 1968 comedy Padosan, Kishore Kumar played a music teacher and sang in a memorable musical 'duel' against a character voiced by which singer?
questions.explanation: In 'Ek Chatur Naar' from Padosan, Kishore Kumar's voice spars with the classical-style singing of Manna Dey.

questions.q: Which early singing idol did the young Kishore Kumar idolise and initially imitate before developing his own style?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was a devoted admirer of K. L. Saigal and modelled his early singing on Saigal's voice.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar's distinctive vocal technique of yodelling was notably heard in songs from which of his films?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar showcased his trademark yodelling in the title song of Jhumroo (1961).

questions.q: Apart from R. D. Burman, with which veteran composer (R. D.'s father) did Kishore Kumar record many memorable songs?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar recorded numerous classics with S. D. Burman, the father of R. D. Burman.

questions.q: In which year did the singer and actor Kishore Kumar die?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar died on 13 October 1987 in Mumbai.

questions.q: The well-known Kishore Kumar song 'Roop Tera Mastana' was filmed on which screen couple in Aradhana?
questions.explanation: 'Roop Tera Mastana' was picturised on Rajesh Khanna and Sharmila Tagore in Aradhana (1969).

questions.q: Which music director gave Kishore Kumar one of his earliest film songs and encouraged his singing career in the late 1940s?
questions.explanation: Composer Khemchand Prakash gave Kishore Kumar an important early break with the song 'Marne Ki Duayen Kyon Mangu' in Ziddi (1948).

questions.q: Kishore Kumar's energetic song 'Yeh Dosti' (about friendship) was a duet with which singer for the 1975 film Sholay?
questions.explanation: 'Yeh Dosti' from Sholay (1975) was sung by Kishore Kumar and Manna Dey, composed by R. D. Burman.

questions.q: During the Emergency in the mid-1970s, Kishore Kumar's songs were reportedly banned on which state broadcaster after he declined to perform at a government event?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar's songs were banned on All India Radio during the Emergency after he refused to sing for a Congress government function.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar produced and directed the 1964 film about a struggling musician, considered one of his most personal works. What was its title?
questions.explanation: Door Gagan Ki Chhaon Mein (1964) was written, produced, directed by and starred Kishore Kumar.

questions.q: Which composer created the Kishore Kumar song 'Hazaar Raahein Mud Ke Dekhin' from the 1980 film Thodisi Bewafaii?
questions.explanation: 'Hazaar Raahein Mud Ke Dekhin' from Thodisi Bewafaii (1980) was composed by Khayyam.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar was married four times; which actress was among his wives and starred opposite him in films?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar's wives included the actress Madhubala, whom he married in 1960.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar sang playback in several languages besides Hindi; he recorded a notable body of work in which language, his mother tongue?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar, born into a Bengali family, recorded numerous songs in Bengali, his mother tongue.

questions.q: Which 1972 film, in which Kishore Kumar sang for Rajesh Khanna, featured the melancholic R. D. Burman composition 'Chingari Koi Bhadke'?
questions.explanation: 'Chingari Koi Bhadke' is from Amar Prem (1972), sung by Kishore Kumar with music by R. D. Burman.

questions.q: Kishore Kumar was a favoured playback voice for which actor, singing for him in S. D. Burman-scored films such as Guide (1965)?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was a preferred playback singer for Dev Anand, voicing him in films including Guide (1965), scored by S. D. Burman.

questions.q: The Madhya Pradesh government instituted an award in Kishore Kumar's honour, the Kishore Kumar Samman, recognising contributions to which field?
questions.explanation: The Madhya Pradesh government's Kishore Kumar Samman honours outstanding contributions to Indian cinema and the arts.
